heading: Custom Storage Speed
body:
Atlas measures storage speed as maximum IOPS (Input/Output Operations per Second). Each Atlas
[cluster tier](instance-size-costs) offers a default storage
speed that is included in the cluster's per-hour cost. The choice of
cloud service provider and cluster affects the available storage
speed customization options, as well as the cost of selecting a custom
storage speed.

**Aws:**

AWS

For most cluster types, you can increase storage speed from
**Standard** to **Fast** or **Fastest**,
which affects costs. Selecting a custom speed changes both IOPS (Input/Output Operations per Second)
and the type of storage used. The storage type changes from a
general-purpose SSD (Solid State Disk) to a provisioned-IOPS (Input/Output Operations per Second) SSD (Solid State Disk).
To learn more about storage types, see
[Amazon EBS Volume Types](http://docs.aws.amazon.com/AWSEC2/latest/UserGuide/EBSVolumeTypes.html).

**Azure:**

Azure

All clusters use premium SSD (Solid State Disk) disks with IOPS (Input/Output Operations per Second) based on the
storage capacity. The maximum IOPS (Input/Output Operations per Second) increases as
you increase storage capacity for the cluster in the same tier.
The cost of the increased IOPS (Input/Output Operations per Second) is included in the cost of the
increased storage capacity. If you choose to increase IOPS (Input/Output Operations per Second) in
addition to the increased IOPS (Input/Output Operations per Second) that comes with the increased
storage capacity, MongoDB charges you more.

To learn more, see [Extend Storage Capacity and IOPS on Azure](change-storage-capacity-azure)
and [High-performance Premium Storage and Managed Disks for VMs](https://docs.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/storage/storage-premium-storage).

**Gcp:**

GCP

All clusters use SSD (Solid State Disk) persistent disks with fixed maximum IOPS (Input/Output Operations per Second)
based on the cluster storage capacity. The maximum IOPS (Input/Output Operations per Second)
increases as storage capacity increases. The cost of the
increased maximum IOPS (Input/Output Operations per Second) is included in the cost of the increased
storage capacity. To learn more about the Google Cloud persistent disks,
see [Persistent Disks](https://cloud.google.com/compute/docs/disks/#pdspecs).
